entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
The discussion focuses on the strategic decisions that miners must make in blockchain transaction scenarios, particularly when considering whether to wait for a transaction fee. The analysis hinges on the distinction between myopic (short-term focused) and non-myopic (long-term focused) miners. Non-myopic miners, who are rational, will wait for even a small fee as indicated by the mad-htlc result. Conversely, if surrounded by myopic miners, a non-myopic miner should wait only for a significantly larger fee. This scenario's dynamics depend on factors such as miner sizes and the timeout settings.
The implications of these strategies were previously explored in a research paper by Winzer, Herd, and Faust, which found that the required bribe to secure cooperation from miners grows exponentially with the length of the timeout. This finding aligns closely with another study by Wizner et al., emphasizing the risks for attackers as the costs escalate. However, the mad-htlc approach provides a strategic advantage by eliminating the reliance on myopic assumptions, thereby enhancing contract security under various conditions.
For those interested in a deeper exploration of these concepts, further details can be found in the paper titled "Presence of Rational Miners" published during the 2019 IEEE European Symposium on Security and Privacy Workshops. The paper is accessible [here](https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/8802377).
